canonical_layer_boundary: customer_risk_profiles table in riskdb Postgres database

nodes.name: CustomerRiskProfile
nodes.annotation: Single promoted entity within the declared canonical layer boundary. Represents a pre-assessed customer risk record. Risk data is loaded at initialisation time (M-007); the API provides read-only access only — no write paths exist through any API route.

nodes.name: customer_id
nodes.annotation: Natural key — business-assigned identifier. CUST-NNN format observed consistently across seed data and test fixtures (CUST-001 through CUST-009 in seed; CUST-999 as test sentinel for non-existent customer). VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY — no DB-level constraint enforces the CUST-NNN format; any non-null string is structurally valid. Annotation should confirm: (1) whether CUST-NNN is a system-assigned format or externally supplied; (2) whether the 3-digit space is bounded in practice.

nodes.name: risk_tier
nodes.annotation: Controlled vocabulary — three-value closed set (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH). Doubly enforced: CHECK constraint in DDL and VALID_TIERS set in test_invariants.py. No ordering declared in schema; LOW < MEDIUM < HIGH ordering is implied by naming only — annotation candidate (see F02 naming pattern flags). Vocabulary linked to SV-001.

nodes.name: risk_factors
nodes.disambiguation_note: NOT NULL constraint prevents null array but permits empty array {}. Whether an empty risk_factors array is a valid business state (e.g. newly-onboarded customer with no assessed factors) is NOT DETERMINABLE from schema or seed data alone. All 9 seed rows have non-empty arrays (2–3 elements). Annotation required. UK English spelling used consistently in seed factor strings (utilisation, not utilization) — confirm intended locale for factor string authoring.
nodes.annotation: Free-form text array — not a controlled vocabulary. 21 unique factor strings observed across 9 seed rows; no string appears across tiers. Factor strings are plain English phrases authored externally to the system (the API only reads them). No enum constraint at DB level.

nodes.name: risk_tier_vocabulary
nodes.annotation: Vocabulary for the risk_tier field on CustomerRiskProfile. Three-value closed set. Source authority: CHECK constraint in db/init.sql and VALID_TIERS in test_invariants.py — both must be kept in sync on any future vocabulary change.

3 seed rows. Observed factors indicate customers in good standing with no adverse indicators. Annotation should confirm: whether LOW is a terminal state (customer relationship closed) or whether tier changes are operationally expected.

3 seed rows. Observed factors indicate elevated but not severe indicators (late payments, moderate utilisation, partial verification). Annotation should confirm: whether MEDIUM represents a transient or stable state in the customer lifecycle.

3 seed rows. Observed factors indicate serious adverse indicators (collections, fraud alert, default, county court judgement). Annotation should confirm: whether HIGH tier is re-assessed on a schedule, triggered by events, or set-and-held. No transition history available in single-table read-only schema.
